---
title: "Assignment 2"
format: html
theme: sandstone
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
toc-float: yes
editor: source
---

Andrew Steinkruger  
AEC 699  
April 18, 2026  

## Header

Text

```{r packages, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}

library(openxlsx)
library(magrittr)
library(tibble)
library(janitor)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gt)
library(forcats)
library(ggthemes)
library(scales)
library(ggtext)
library(plotly)

```

## Data

Get EIA.

```{r data}

url <- "https://www.eia.gov/coal/archive/coal_historical_exports.xlsx"
path <- "../data/coal_historical_exports.csv"
check <- file.exists(path)

if(check){
  dat_coal <- read.csv(path)
  message("Read data from disk.")
}else{
  dat_coal <- read.xlsx(url, startRow = 4, na.strings = ".")
  write.csv(dat_coal, path, row.names = FALSE)
  message("Read data from web, then wrote data to disk.")
}

rm(url, path, check)

```
## (1) Fixing Column Names

Fix column names. 

```{r names}

dat_coal_names = dat_coal %>% as_tibble %>% clean_names

```

## (2) Annual Coal Exports

Plot annual coal exports. 

```{r vis_1}

vis_1 = 
  dat_coal_names %>%
  mutate(total_millions = total * 1e-6,
         total_revenue_billions = total_revenue * 1e-9) %>% 
  select(year, 
         total_millions, 
         total_revenue_billions) %>% 
  pivot_longer(-year) %>% 
  group_by(year, name) %>% 
  summarize(value = value %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  ggplot() + 
  geom_col(aes(x = year %>% factor,
               y = value,
               fill = name)) + 
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = c("2002 Q1", "2013 Q3", "2025 Q4")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) + 
  facet_wrap(~ name, 
             scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# export and reference in markdown

```

## (3) Quarterly Coal Exports

Plot quarterly coal exports. 

```{r vis_2}

vis_2 = 
  dat_coal_names %>% 
  mutate(total_millions = total * 1e-6,
         total_revenue_billions = total_revenue * 1e-9,
         yearquarter = paste0(year, " Q", quarter)) %>% 
  select(yearquarter, 
         total_millions, 
         total_revenue_billions) %>% 
  pivot_longer(-yearquarter) %>% 
  mutate(name_factor = 
           ifelse(name == "total_millions", 
                  "Exports (Short Tons, Millions)",
                  "Exports (Nominal US$, Billions)") %>% 
           factor) %>% 
  group_by(yearquarter, name) %>% 
  summarize(value = value %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  ggplot() + 
  geom_col(aes(x = yearquarter %>% factor,
               y = value,
               fill = name)) + 
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = c("2002 Q1", "2013 Q3", "2025 Q4")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) + 
  facet_wrap(~ name, 
             scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# export and reference in markdown

# note date handling not required

```

Describe date handling without being annoying. 

Decompose the time series to get a seasonal trend out visually. 

```{r seasonality}

vis_seasonality = 
  dat_coal_names %>% 
  group_by(year, quarter) %>% 
  summarize(total_millions = total %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE) %>% multiply_by(1e-6),
            total_revenue_billions = total_revenue %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE) %>% multiply_by(1e-9),
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("total")) %>% 
  ggplot() + 
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = quarter %>% factor,
                   y = value,
                   color = name)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) + 
  facet_wrap(~ name,
             scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

## (4) Exports by Destination

### (4.1) 

```{r data_41}

coal_country = 
  dat_coal_names %>% 
  group_by(year, quarter, coal_destination_country) %>% 
  summarize(total = total %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE),
            total_revenue = total_revenue %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup

coal_country %>% head(10) %>% gt

```

### (4.2)

```{r data_42}

# What number of countries should we observe in each quarter?
#  This will be useful later.

int_country = 
  coal_country %>% 
  pull(coal_destination_country) %>% 
  unique %>% 
  length

# What number of quarters should we observe for each country?
#  Note that we could be missing years (although we're not). 

int_length = 
  coal_country %>% 
  pull(year) %>% 
  unique %>% 
  {seq(min(.), max(.))} %>%  
  length %>% 
  multiply_by(4)

# What number of observations do we observe for each country?

coal_country_missing = 
  coal_country %>% 
  group_by(coal_destination_country) %>% 
  summarize(observations = n()) %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  mutate(check = (observations == int_length),
         proportion = observations / int_length)

coal_country_missing %>% head(10) %>% gt

# What's the distribution of observation missingness by country?

vis_missing = 
  coal_country_missing %>% 
  ggplot() +
  geom_histogram(aes(x = proportion))

vis_missing

```

### (4.3)

```{r data_43}

# Complete the data with missing observations by country, year, and quarter. 

coal_country_complete = 
  coal_country %>% 
  complete(year, quarter, coal_destination_country) %>% 
  arrange(coal_destination_country, year, quarter)

# Check whether the data are complete. 

int_obs_expected = 
  coal_country %>% 
  pull(coal_destination_country) %>% 
  unique %>% 
  length %>% 
  multiply_by(int_length)

int_obs_actual = 
  coal_country_complete %>% 
  nrow

int_obs_actual == int_obs_expected

# Show off the complete data. 

coal_country_complete %>% head(10) %>% gt

```

### (4.4)

```{r data_44}

# Check whether there is a quarter with no non-missing observations. 

coal_country_complete %>% 
  mutate(missing = is.na(total)) %>% 
  group_by(year, quarter) %>% 
  summarize(missing = sum(missing),
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  mutate(check = (missing == int_country)) %>% 
  filter(check) %>% 
  nrow

```
### (4.5)

```{r data_45}

# Check the top ten importers of coal from the United States. 

#  Tonnage

coal10_culm = 
  coal_country %>% 
  group_by(coal_destination_country) %>% 
  summarize(total = total %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  arrange(desc(total)) %>% 
  slice(1:10)

coal10_culm %>% gt

#  Value

coal10_value = 
  coal_country %>% 
  group_by(coal_destination_country) %>% 
  summarize(total_revenue = total_revenue %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  arrange(desc(total_revenue)) %>% 
  slice(1:10)

coal10_value %>% gt

```

### (4.6)

```{r data_46}

# Check the top ten importers of coal from the United States for 2025 Q4. 

#  Tonnage

coal10_tonnage_recent = 
  coal_country %>% 
  filter(year == max(year)) %>% 
  filter(quarter == max(quarter)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(total)) %>% 
  slice(1:10)

coal10_tonnage_recent %>% gt

```

### (4.7)

```{r vis_47}

vis_top = 
  coal_country %>% 
  left_join(coal10_culm %>% 
              mutate(rank = row_number()) %>% 
              select(-total)) %>% 
  mutate(yearquarter = paste0(year, " Q", quarter),
         country = coal_destination_country, 
         total_millions = total * 1e-6,
         rank = rank %>% as.character %>% replace_na("Other"),
         .keep = "none") %>% 
  group_by(yearquarter, rank) %>% 
  summarize(total_millions = total_millions %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE),
            country = country %>% max) %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  mutate(country = ifelse(rank == "Other", "Other", country),
         country = country %>% 
           factor %>% 
           fct_reorder(.x = total_millions,
                       .fun = max,
                       .desc = TRUE)) %>% 
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = yearquarter %>% factor,
                y = total_millions,
                group = country)) + 
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = c("2002 Q1", "2013 Q3", "2025 Q4")) +
  labs(y = "U.S. Coal Exports (Short Tons, Millions)") + 
  facet_wrap(~ country, 
             scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

vis_top

```

### (4.8)

```{r vis_48}

# Replicate the previous figure with more interesting visualization tools. 

vis_top_better = 
  dat_coal_names %>% 
  select(year, 
         quarter, 
         country = coal_destination_country,
         tonnage_steam = steam_coal,
         tonnage_metal = metallurgical,
         tonnage_coke = coke) %>% 
  group_by(year, quarter, country) %>% 
  summarize(across(starts_with("tonnage"), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  complete(year, quarter, country) %>% 
  arrange(country, year, quarter) %>% 
  mutate(yearquarter = paste0(year, " Q", quarter)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("tonnage")) %>% 
  mutate(value = value * 1e-6,
         product = 
           case_when(name == "tonnage_steam" ~ "Steam Coal",
                     name == "tonnage_metal" ~ "Metallurgical Coal",
                     TRUE ~ "Coke")) %>% 
  left_join(coal10_culm %>% 
              filter(row_number() %in% 1:5) %>% 
              mutate(rank = row_number()) %>% 
              select(-total),
            by = c("country" = "coal_destination_country")) %>% 
  mutate(rank = rank %>% as.character %>% replace_na("Other")) %>% 
  group_by(yearquarter, 
           rank,
           product) %>% 
  summarize(value = value %>% sum(na.rm = TRUE),
            country = country %>% max,
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  mutate(country = 
           case_when(rank == "Other" ~ "Other",
                     country == "South Korea (Republic of Korea)" ~ "South Korea",
                     country == "Germany, Federal Republic of" ~ "Germany",
                     TRUE ~ country) %>% 
           factor %>% 
           fct_reorder(.x = value,
                       .fun = sum,
                       .desc = TRUE) %>% 
           fct_relevel("Other",
                       after = Inf),
         product = 
           product %>% 
           factor %>% 
           fct_rev,
         yearquarter = yearquarter %>% factor) %>% 
  # Add human-facing names for plotly. 
  rename(`Short Tons, Millions` = value,
         `Year and Quarter` = yearquarter,
         Product = product) %>% 
  ggplot() + 
  geom_line(aes(x = `Year and Quarter`,
                y = `Short Tons, Millions`,
                group = Product,
                color = Product),
            linewidth = 1.00,
            alpha = 0.75) + 
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = c("2002 Q1", "2013 Q3", "2025 Q4")) +
  facet_wrap(~ country,
             ncol = 1, 
             nrow = 6,
             scales = "free_y",
             strip.position = "right") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
  scale_color_solarized() + 
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Short Tons, Millions",
       color = NULL,
       title = "Quarterly U.S. coal exports to top 5 destinations, 2002-2025",
       caption = "Data: U.S. Energy Information Administration, *Quarterly Coal Report, 2026*") +
  theme_solarized() +
  theme(axis.text.x = 
          element_text(angle = 45, 
                       vjust = 1.0, 
                       hjust = 1.0),
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.direction = "horizontal",
        plot.caption = element_markdown())

ggsave("../out/vis_top_better.png",
       vis_top_better,
       dpi = 300,
       width = 6.5,
       height = 9)

ggsave("vis_top_better.png",
       vis_top_better,
       dpi = 300,
       width = 6.5,
       height = 9)

```

![](vis_top_better.png){fig-alt="A prettier plot of export destinations." fig-align="center" width="100%"}

### (4.9)

Note that plotly does not support horizontal legends.

Drop it for duplicated information. 

Also note that plotly is no longer actively supporting R. 

```{r vis_49}

ggplotly(vis_top_better + theme(legend.position = "none"),
         width = 750,
         height = 900)

```

## (5)

Is there an obvious relationship between prices and quantities by product?

```{r}

vis_5 = 
  dat_coal_names %>% 
  select(year, 
         quarter, 
         customs_district, 
         coal_destination_country,
         q_steam = steam_coal,
         q_metal = metallurgical,
         q_coke = coke,
         pq_steam = steam_revenue,
         pq_metal = metallurgical_revenue,
         pq_coke = coke_revenue) %>% 
  group_by(year,
           quarter,
           coal_destination_country) %>% 
  summarize(across(starts_with(c("q_", "pq_")), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop_last") %>% 
  ungroup %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with(c("q_", "pq_")),
               names_sep = "_",
               names_to = c("var", "product")) %>% 
  drop_na %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = var,
              values_from = value) %>% 
  mutate(p = pq / q) %>% 
  drop_na %>% 
  # filter(p > quantile(p, 0.05) & p < quantile(p, 0.95)) %>% 
  # filter(q > quantile(q, 0.05) & q < quantile(q, 0.95)) %>% 
  ggplot() + 
  geom_point(aes(x = q,
                 y = p)) +
  facet_wrap(~ product,
             scales = "free")

```
